behaviors.description: All direct and transitive dependencies are extracted from the manifest and included in the vulnerability check.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is parser/resolver/query handoff across modules (MOCK-MISS). A unit test of only manifest parsing would miss dropped transitive deps during resolver->query payload assembly. No real OS/network/subprocess is required (REAL-INFRA no), and no AI output dependency (LLM-DEP no). Rejected System: behavior text does not require real HTTP/OS, only inclusion correctness.
behaviors.test_description: Wire manifest reader + dependency graph resolver + query-builder with fixture manifests (including transitives), and a stub vulnerability client that records requested package@version entries. Assert every expected direct/transitive dependency is present in outgoing checks.

behaviors.description: The vulnerability database query returns results that match the exact version ranges in the manifest, not just the package name.
behaviors.justification: This targets request/response contract between version-range extraction and query/filter logic across modules. Unit tests for just range parsing or just filtering can miss mismatch at the boundary. Rejected System: no explicit need for real network behavior; an in-process stub can validate exact range parameters and range-aware matching semantics. No AI dependency.
behaviors.test_description: Use fixtures with same package name but multiple vulnerable/non-vulnerable version ranges. Run scanner query path against a stub DB service/client that returns range-sensitive CVE data; assert only CVEs matching manifest ranges are included.

behaviors.description: The severity sorting places Critical above High above Medium above Low, with CVEs of the same severity sorted by CVSS score.
behaviors.justification: Behavior is pure ordering logic in one local boundary; no module handoff or I/O is required. Integration/System would not catch additional real-failure modes beyond deterministic comparator correctness.
behaviors.test_description: Feed an unsorted in-memory CVE list spanning all severities and tied severities with different CVSS values into the sort function/object. Assert exact ordered output: severity rank first, CVSS descending within same severity.

behaviors.description: The manifest modification changes only the affected dependency's version, leaving all other entries unchanged.
behaviors.justification: Real failure appears at parse->edit->serialize pipeline boundary: a local edit function may be correct while writer reorders/rewrites unrelated entries. No real OS/network/subprocess needed; can run in-process on fixture files/strings. Rejected System: behavior does not require filesystem event/OS semantics, only content integrity.
behaviors.test_description: Run manifest updater end-to-end through parser and serializer on representative manifests. Assert diff contains exactly one dependency version change and byte-for-byte (or canonical-structure-for-structure) equality for all other entries.

behaviors.description: The auto-generated pull request is created on the real repository with the correct single-dependency diff.
behaviors.justification: Grounded evidence: behavior explicitly says "created on the real repository". This requires real external API/git/subprocess/remote-repo semantics (REAL-INFRA yes). Lower-level miss-proof: mocked PR client can pass while real auth scopes, branch protections, commit/push behavior, or server-side diff generation fail (MOCK-HIDE). Rejected Agentic/Workflow: no LLM/model decision or multi-agent chain in behavior text.
behaviors.test_description: Against a real test repository, run scanner auto-PR flow to create branch/commit/push/PR. Assert PR exists remotely and diff contains exactly one dependency version change with no unrelated file changes.

behaviors.description: A major version bump is correctly identified as a breaking change based on semver comparison.
behaviors.justification: Semver major-bump detection is local deterministic logic (single function/object boundary). No I/O or module interaction required to expose the core failure.
behaviors.test_description: Table-drive semver pairs (including pre-release/build metadata edge cases) into breaking-change detector and assert true only when major version increases per semver rules.

behaviors.description: When a breaking change is detected, PR creation is skipped and a review flag is surfaced in the scanner output.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is orchestration/state handoff between semver detector, PR creation gate, and output/report module. Unit-testing only gate logic can miss incorrect wiring where PR client is still invoked or flag not propagated. Rejected System: behavior does not require real remote PR call; a spy/stub PR client is sufficient to verify "not called" plus output flag. No AI dependency or multi-agent sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Run scanner flow with an update classified as breaking, wiring real decision/report modules and a stub PR client. Assert PR creation path is not invoked and scanner output includes the review-required flag.
